bead.title: tracker: auto-close epics when all children reach a terminal state
bead.description:
PROBLEM: epic beads remain status=open even after every child has reached a terminal state (closed or cancelled). Operators have to manually run ddx bead close <epic-id> for each one, which is easy to forget and creates a steady accumulation of "open" beads that are actually done.

Observed 2026-05-31 in /Users/erik/Projects/helix — 6 epics were sitting open with 100% terminal-state children:

  helix-88f790e4  Epic: HELIX workflow coverage across 5 runtimes (FEAT-014)      25/25 closed
  helix-fef22846  Audit deleted HELIX artifact types                                25/25 closed
  helix-45815a4d  Epic: harden Claude Code install surface                          10 closed + 1 cancelled
  helix-db7d13a9  Worked example: HELIX driving CRM design                           4 closed + 1 cancelled
  helix-e1a72cfc  Epic: Audit and iterate the full HELIX microsite                  19/19 closed
  helix-e3c7d0e4  Epic: per-runtime integration tests                                5/5 closed

PROPOSED BEHAVIOR: when the last non-terminal child of an epic transitions to closed/cancelled, ddx auto-closes the parent epic. Conservative options to discuss:

1. Treat closed AND cancelled as terminal for parent-close purposes (current data suggests both should count — cancelled children clearly aren't returning).
2. Make this opt-in via a label or epic-kind flag, so people can still keep an epic open after children land if there's follow-up they want to track on the epic itself.
3. Auto-emit a `closing_commit_sha` derived from the last child's closing commit, OR leave it null with a "auto-closed: all children terminal" note.

If auto-close is too aggressive, a softer alternative: a `ddx bead reap` / `ddx bead check` command that prints epics whose children are all terminal and offers a one-shot close.

NON-SCOPE: detecting whether an epic SHOULD have been decomposed differently — this is purely about closing the parent once its planned children all land.
bead.acceptance:
1. When the last non-terminal child of an epic (parent bead with at least one child) transitions to a terminal state via `ddx bead close`, the epic auto-closes IF policy is "auto" (decided in design phase).
2. If policy is "manual" (current behavior), `ddx bead reap` (or equivalent) lists every epic whose children are all terminal, and `--apply` closes them in a batch.
3. New tests exercise: (a) epic with mixed open/closed children stays open; (b) epic with all-closed children either auto-closes or is reported by reap; (c) "cancelled" children count as terminal; (d) epics with no children are left alone (no false positives).
4. Behavior is documented in `docs/beads.md` (or wherever the bead lifecycle is documented).
5. `go test ./...` passes for the bead package.

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"implementation_path_unselected: The bead is not executable as written because it leaves the core product choice unresolved: it proposes both auto-close and manual reap/check paths, and it defers the deciding policy to a later design phase. It also leaves terminal-state semantics and closing_commit_sha behavior open, so an implementer cannot start without first narrowing the scope.","fingerprint":"22466983d4893b09874e96a043973070d915ad30e373ef0129f56a6e0ab3992e","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"22466983d4893b09874e96a043973070d915ad30e373ef0129f56a6e0ab3992e"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"design decision unresolved: auto-close vs. reap command vs. both: The bead presents three mutually exclusive implementation options without resolving which to pursue. AC#1 explicitly defers to a 'design phase' that has not occurred, making the acceptance criteria conditionally defined. An agent claiming this bead cannot determine whether to extend cascadeAndWalkUp in store.go:1617 to cover epics, add a new 'ddx bead reap' command, or both. The gap in the existing code is locatable and narrow — walkUpClosureCandidate at store.go:1620 skips execution-eligible parents and only counts StatusClosed (not StatusCancelled) as terminal — but the policy decision (auto vs. manual+reap) must be made before implementation begins.","fingerprint":"fd72fb82d692305891fccfb8a10fd7bc313ac8449184cc4e89546dd4fb9e8769","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"fd72fb82d692305891fccfb8a10fd7bc313ac8449184cc4e89546dd4fb9e8769"}
